ground_truth.title: 12 eBPF-Powered CLI Utilities That Every Modern Linux Sysadmin Should Master
ground_truth.author: Neville Ondara
ground_truth.main_content: Classic Linux tools are always there, but eBPF is the natural upgrade as it looks directly in the kernel. Once you start using these tools, it's hard to imagine managing Linux systems without them. This article covers 12 essential eBPF-powered CLI tools for Linux sysadmins including bpftrace (the ultimate custom debugger for one-off investigations), execsnoop (catches fleeting processes that spawn and die too fast for ps), opensnoop (reveals what files processes try to open with exact error codes), tcplife (shows TCP session durations for microservice latency debugging), gethostlatency (traces DNS resolution delays), biotop (real-time disk I/O process monitoring), biolatency (I/O latency histogram analysis), tcpretrans (spots TCP retransmissions), tcpconnect (traces outbound connections for security), tcpaccept (traces inbound connections), biosnoop (detailed block I/O operations), and bpftool (inspects running eBPF programs). Prerequisites include Linux kernel 4.1+, specific kernel config options, and root privileges. Installation on Debian/Ubuntu requires: sudo apt install -y bpfcc-tools bpftrace linux-tools-common linux-tools-$(uname -r).
with: eBPF (Extended Berkeley Packet Filter) lets you attach tiny, sandboxed programs directly to the Linux kernel, no restarts, no recompiling, no intrusive overhead.
Some processes spawn and die so fast that ps never has a chance to show them. Cron jobs, failing scripts, runaway children, execsnoop catches them all.
Half of the 'network is slow' problems I've debugged were actually DNS delays. gethostlatency traces every hostname resolution.
If your system feels sluggish, disk I/O is often the real culprit, not CPU, not RAM. biotop gives you a real-time, updating list of processes doing disk reads/writes.
Without the right kernel flags like CONFIG_BPF, CONFIG_BPF_SYSCALL, and CONFIG_BPF_JIT, you'll see errors like 'failed to load BPF program' or 'unknown map type'.
without: With the FOSS Weekly Newsletter, you learn useful Linux tips
